channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Players 1, umpires 0: Cubs hurler Cody Poteet makes first ABS challenge in spring training and wins
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Chicago Cubs pitcher Cody Poteet believed the 95 mph fastball he threw to Los Angeles Dodgers slugger Max Muncy was in the strike zone. Plate umpire Tony Randazzo disagreed and called it a ball.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 22 for a new SUV as Juan Soto gives Mets 3B Brett Baty a Tahoe for switching numbers
item.description: PORT ST. LUCIE, Fla. (AP) — Juan Soto got his desired No. 22 jersey with the New York Mets after his record $765 million contract this offseason. Brett Baty, who wore that number the past three seasons, got a brand new SUV in spring training.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ja Morant the Memphis player burglarized by group targeting pro athletes, report says
item.description: A published report says two-time NBA All-Star Ja Morant is the Memphis Grizzlies player whose home was burglarized by the men charged with orchestrating break-ins at the homes of prominent professional athletes around the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tiger Woods joins another White House meeting as PGA Tour moves closer to Saudi deal
item.description: Tiger Woods joined PGA Tour Commissioner Jay Monahan and player director Adam Scott in a second White House meeting on Thursday with President Donald Trump, another sign the sport is moving rapidly toward ending the division brought on by Saudi-funded LIV Golf.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kristin Juszczyk and Kylie Kelce try to embrace being called WAGs
item.description: On her podcast, Kylie Kelce talks to Kristin Juszczyk about the negative connotations of an early 2000s British term that became popular again after Taylor Swift began dating Travis Kelce.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: 4 Nations Face-Off is the latest in a long line of memorable international hockey competitions
item.description: The 4 Nations Face-Off has already delivered an instant classic international hockey game with the fight-filled preliminary round matchup between the United States and Canada last week in Montreal. The U.S.-Canada rematch on Thursday night in Boston could be another memorable country vs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers star Deebo Samuel to host football camp at Bakersfield High School in June
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- San Francisco 49ers wide receiver Deebo Samuel is scheduled to teach the youth of Bakersfield at a football camp. Deebo Samuel's Youth Football Camp plans on coming to town June 13 to give kids hands-on football instruction and teach them more about football.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Eyraud, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cubs announce Shota Imanaga as Opening Day starter in Tokyo
item.description: The Cubs have named their 2025 Opening Day starter, and it's about the least surprising announcement possible.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Researchers confirm Hall of Famer Bobby Hull had CTE when he died 2 years ago
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Bobby Hull, the Hall of Fame forward and two-time NHL MVP who helped the Chicago Blackhawks win the Stanley Cup in 1961, had chronic traumatic encephalopathy when he died two years ago, his widow said in a statement released on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Angels star Mike Trout moving from center to right field in hopes of staying more healthy
item.description: TEMPE, Ariz. (AP) — Los Angeles Angels oft-injured star Mike Trout is moving from center to right field in hopes of better preserving his health. The three-time AL MVP played in 82 or fewer games in three of the past four seasons while dealing with an assortment of injuries.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Aggies lose heartbreaker at first place New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE (ABC4 Sports) - With first place in the Mountain West men’s basketball standings on the line, Utah State (22-4, 12-3 MW) fell just short on Sunday in Albuquerque, dropping an 82-79 thriller at The Pit. This marked the second time in two weeks the Aggies have lost to the Lobos.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NASCAR disqualifies Kligerman for too-low truck after flag-planting win at Daytona. He is appealing
item.description: Full-time television analyst and part-time NASCAR driver Parker Kligerman claimed the checkered flag for the Truck Series season opener and promptly planted it in the grass by the Daytona International Speedway logo.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Final field of 41 set for Daytona 500; Bubba Wallace, Austin Cindric win Duels
item.description: DAYTONA, Fla. (WGHP) — The field of 41 racers has officially been set for Sunday's Daytona 500. This Sunday at 1:30 p.m., the NASCAR series kicks off with the legendary Daytona 500 at Daytona International Speedway in Daytona Beach, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WGN 9

item.title: Two-time All-Star Joey Gallo agrees to minor league contract with the White Sox
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Two-time All-Star Joey Gallo agreed to terms on a minor league contract with the Chicago White Sox on Thursday that includes a non-roster invite to spring training.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
